subject: Geology
title: Unconformities
description: A fast breakdown of Unconformities for builders.

hook.text: A gap in the rock record means millions of years went missing. Erosion erased them. That gap has a name.

buildup.text: Unconformities are surfaces where rock layers were eroded away before new sediment was deposited on top.

discovery.text: There are three types: angular, disconformity, and nonconformity, each revealing a different history.

twist.text: Hutton's famous unconformity at Siccar Point showed that Earth's history was far longer than anyone imagined.

climax.text: Unconformities are the blank pages in Earth's story. They tell us what was destroyed, not just what survived.

punchline.text: Missing pages tell a story too.

quiz.question: What is an unconformity?
options: A gap in the rock record where layers were eroded
